calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Neil Rones. Esq. for Petitioner and Tamera Brown, Legal Assistant

This matter comes on calendar for a Report of Administration and Petition of Payment of Compensation to Attorney and to Administrator for Ordinary Services and for Compensation to Attorney for Extraordinary Services and to hold a reserve and final distribution and waiver of account. This matter was last on calendar in November. The Court continued the matter to allow a supplement for specific information and limit this hearing to any discussion on that information. The Court indicates that the supplemental declaration amended the proposed distribution. The Court rules that the supplemental declaration is an amendment and will waive the notice defect at this time. There is a verified E-Signature by the administrator that refers to the petition rather than the supplement. The Court will waive that defect as well. Counsel has nothing further to add re the substance of the supplement. Counsel advises the Court that they were unable to deliver courtesy copies of the corrected verification of Diane Armstrong prior to the hearing, due to the time of the filing. Counsel was unable to secure a courier service after 3:00 pm. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Jones, Deputy: N. Ng. Matter Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-24-2020 Continued To Dec-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Neil Rones, esq. and Tammara Brown, Legal Assistant.

Mr. Rones stated the response to Nov. 18th notes for issue #3 was incorrect, that he would like to file a supplement to correct it, that he thought the difference was due to amount in reserve, that he realized yesterday morning that was not the case and requested a short continuance via email. The Court stated this was such a straight forward estate, doesn't understand why it's been evolving the way it has and asked what the cash on hand is. Mr. Rones responded cash on hand is $281,939.80, that he would like to file a supplemental declaration from the administrator on expenses she paid out and to submit a new proposed order. Ms. Brown stated the distribution to heirs was revised from $131,189.98 to $130,165.97, that it is less than $1,000.00 change because of administrator spent $2,024.02 for the estate not recorded in the petition. Ms. Brown further stated that she spoke with the administrator yesterday, that the administrator cannot provide the exact total she spent due to a broken water pipe which destroyed the entire first floor of her home, that the 3 expenditures are 1)bond, 2)filing fee 3) administrator did not explain. The Court grants counsel's request for a continuance, to allow a supplement to be filed by December 18, 2020, stated that the only subject at the next hearing are these costs and asked if there is anything else to address now. Mr. Rones stated there is nothing else.

The Report of Administrator and [Petition] for Payment of Compensation to Attorney, etc. is continued to December 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for supplement to be filed by

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
